items.ocr_excerpt: (a) Sketch the graph of y = |x - 2|. (b) Solve the inequality |x - 2| < 3x - 4.

items.mapping_note: logarithmic equation using ln laws and exact solution
items.ocr_excerpt: 2 Solve the equation ln 3 + ln(2x + 5) = 2 ln(x + 2). Give your answer in a simplified exact form. [4]

items.mapping_note: root isolation and iterative numerical method
items.ocr_excerpt: (a) By sketching a suitable pair of graphs, show that the equation sec x = 2 − ½x has exactly one root in the interval 0 ≤ x < ½π. (b) Verify by calculation that this root lies between 0.8 and 1. (c) Use the iterative formula x_{n+1} = cos⁻

items.mapping_note: definite integral involving x sec^2 x
items.ocr_excerpt: 4 Find ∫_{1/6 π}^{1/3 π} x sec²x dx. Give your answer in a simplified exact form.

items.mapping_note: trigonometric identity and equation over interval
items.ocr_excerpt: 5 (a) Show that \(\frac{\cos 3x}{\sin x} + \frac{\sin 3x}{\cos x} = 2 \cot 2x\). (b) Hence solve the equation \(\frac{\cos 3x}{\sin x} + \frac{\sin 3x}{\cos x} = 4\), for \(0 < x < \pi\).

items.mapping_note: separable differential equation with limiting behaviour

7 The equation of a curve is x³ + 3xy² − y³ = 5. (a) Show that dy/dx = (x² + y²)/(y² − 2xy). (b) Find the coordinates of the points on the curve where the tangent is parallel to the y-axis. [5]

items.mapping_note: 3D vector geometry in cuboid with line and perpendicular foot

items.mapping_note: partial fractions and power-series expansion of rational function
items.ocr_excerpt: 9 Let f(x) = (2 + 11x - 10x^2) / ((1 + 2x)(1 - 2x)(2 + x)). (a) Express f(x) in partial fractions. (b) Hence obtain the expansion of f(x) in ascending powers of x, up to and including the term in x². [5]

items.mapping_note: complex simultaneous equations and Argand locus
